name: Sesame Chicken Fingers with Plum Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. If the chicken breasts are whole, split them. Cut each breast into half-inch strips.
In a large bowl, combine buttermilk, 2 tablespoons of lemon juice, Worcestershire sauce, soy sauce, paprika, pepper, and garlic. Add chicken strips and toss to coat. Cover and marinate at room temperature for 1 hour, or refrigerate overnight.
2. Preheat the oven to 350°F. Drain the chicken well. In a large shallow bowl, mix bread crumbs with sesame seeds. Roll each chicken strip in the seasoned crumbs until coated. Arrange them in a single layer on a greased baking sheet. Drizzle melted butter over the chicken fingers.
Bake for 35 minutes.
3. Meanwhile, in a nonaluminum saucepan, combine plum preserves, dry mustard, horseradish, and remaining 2 tablespoons of lemon juice. Melt over low heat, stirring until smooth.
4. Serve the chicken fingers hot or warm with the plum sauce for dipping on the side.
